losses_past_5_years_table_row_1.details_of_loss: Frozen pipe in second-floor bathroom burst during an extended cold snap while the family was away for a long weekend. Water leaked through the ceiling into the kitchen below, damaging drywall, insulation, a section of hardwood flooring, and a few base cabinets. No mold developed because mitigation started within 24 hours of discovery. No prior related issues and plumbing has since been partially re-insulated and a smart thermostat with low-temperature alerts installed.

Wind and hail storm caused damage to the asphalt shingle roof on the south-facing slope and broke two double-hung windows. Several shingles were lifted or missing, and there was minor water staining on a bedroom ceiling but no structural damage. Emergency tarping was performed the same day. Full repair and replacement of affected roof section and windows completed within two weeks.

losses_in_past_5_years.details_of_loss_row_1: 1) 2022 frozen pipe water damage claim in bathroom/kitchen; 2) 2020 wind/hail roof and window damage. Both incidents fully repaired with no ongoing issues, no liability claims, and no injuries. Insured responded promptly, cooperated fully with adjusters, and completed recommended mitigation improvements (additional pipe insulation, smart thermostat installation, and roof inspection).
losses_in_past_5_years.open_closed_row_1: Both claims closed with no pending litigation or subrogation. No open claims at this time.
losses_in_past_5_years.details_of_loss_row_2: Frozen pipe burst in second-floor bathroom leading to water damage in bathroom and kitchen ceiling below. Damage limited to interior finishes and some built-in cabinetry; no structural framing compromised. Professional water mitigation and drying performed within 24 hours, followed by replacement of damaged drywall, insulation, paint, and a section of hardwood flooring. Insured paid deductible and also upgraded to more water-resistant flooring in the kitchen at their own expense beyond claim payment.
losses_in_past_5_years.details_of_loss_row_3: Severe thunderstorm with high winds and hail impacted the neighborhood. South-facing roof slope lost several shingles, and hail cracked two double-hung windows on the second floor. Minor interior ceiling staining but no long-term moisture intrusion. Roof section and windows replaced by licensed contractor; full roof was inspected and found otherwise sound. Insured has since had routine roof inspections every other year.

premises.construction_type: Wood frame with vinyl siding and asphalt shingle roof over an unfinished basement.
premises.plumbing_type: Copper supply lines with PVC and ABS drain lines; PEX used for 2022 bathroom repairs.
premises.roof_type: Architectural asphalt shingles installed in 2017 with ice and water shield along eaves.
premises.wiring_type: Modern copper wiring with circuit breaker panel (no aluminum, no knob-and-tube).
premises.heating_type: Forced hot air by natural gas with central air conditioning; programmable smart thermostat.
premises.describe_renovations: No active renovations at this time. Past work includes a kitchen refresh in 2018 (cabinet refacing, new appliances, and countertop replacement) and bathroom repairs in 2022 following the frozen pipe claim. All work was permitted where required and performed by licensed contractors. No structural walls were moved or altered, and there are no open permits.

vacant_only.vacant_how_long: N/A - property is owner-occupied as primary residence with no periods of vacancy exceeding 30 consecutive days in the past 3 years.
vacant_only.intended_use_building: N/A - building is currently used and will continue to be used as an owner-occupied single-family primary residence.

coverages.extended_repl_cost: Requested 25% extended replacement cost on Coverage A (dwelling) to account for potential inflation and local labor/material cost spikes.

additional_information_for_underwriting.additional_info_underwriting_line_1: Named insured, Jonathan M. Reynolds, is a 43-year-old software project manager who has lived at the property since 2014 with his spouse (Emily, 41, part-time speech therapist) and their two children (ages 9 and 12). Both adults are non-smokers, there are no smokers in the household, and there is one family dog (medium-sized mixed breed, approximately 45 lbs, no bite history, not on any restricted breeds list).
additional_information_for_underwriting.additional_info_underwriting_line_2: Home is used strictly as a primary residence with no short-term rentals (no Airbnb/VRBO) and no long-term roomers or boarders. Jonathan occasionally works from a small home office (one room over the garage) for his employer; no clients or customers visit the premises, and no business inventory or specialized equipment is stored at the home beyond typical computer and office equipment. This has been disclosed as incidental office use only. The home is typically occupied in the evenings and on weekends, and a trusted neighbor checks the property when the family is away for more than a couple of days.
additional_information_for_underwriting.additional_info_underwriting_line_3: The property is located in an established suburban neighborhood with sidewalks, street lighting, and a neighborhood watch email group. Fire hydrant is approximately 300 feet from the home, and the responding fire station is about 1.8 miles away. The home has interconnected smoke detectors on every level and in each bedroom, carbon monoxide detectors on each sleeping level, and fire extinguishers in the kitchen and near the garage entry. The insured is proactive about maintenance (annual HVAC service, roof inspections every other year, and regular gutter cleaning) and has expressed strong interest in long-term coverage stability and appropriate limits rather than minimum premium. No known uncorrected hazards, no trampolines, no swimming pool, and no aggressive or exotic animals on the premises.
